example: The autonomous constitutional authority responsible for administering election processes in India.
The device used to record votes electronically instead of using paper ballots.
A printer attached to the EVM that gives a paper slip to verify the vote was cast correctly.
Rules for political parties and candidates to ensure free and fair elections, starting from announcement date.
A geographical area that elects a single representative to a legislative body.
The lower house of India's bicameral Parliament, with members directly elected by the public.
The upper house of Parliament, representing the states and union territories of India.
Candidates must file an affidavit detailing their criminal record, assets, and liabilities.
The formal process where a person submits papers to become an official candidate in the election.

definition: Election official in charge of a constituency
example: The officer who oversees the election process in a constituency, including nominations and counting.
A grassroots official responsible for maintaining the electoral roll of a specific polling booth area.
A ballot option allowing voters to indicate disapproval of all candidates in the election.
A poll of voters taken immediately after they have exited the polling stations to predict the result.
An election held to fill a political office that has become vacant between general elections.

definition: Voting again in a specific booth
example: Ordered by the ECI if there was violence, booth capturing, or EVM failure during the initial voting.
The official in charge of a specific polling booth on election day to ensure voting runs smoothly.
The application form for inclusion of name in the electoral roll for first-time voters.
The official ID card issued by the ECI to eligible voters to establish identity at the polling booth.
A voluntary process to link the 12-digit Aadhaar number with the EPIC to authenticate electoral roll data.
The act of redrawing the boundaries of assembly or Lok Sabha constituencies based on recent census data.
Specific seats where only candidates belonging to Scheduled Castes or Scheduled Tribes can contest.
Punishes MPs/MLAs who leave one party for another after being elected, often resulting in disqualification.
Imposed on a state when the state government is unable to function according to constitutional provisions.
A parliamentary motion moved by the opposition to test if the ruling government still has majority support.
A situation where no single political party or pre-poll alliance secures an absolute majority of seats.
